The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kabuyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=draft}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|12=draft}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kabuyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鹿歩山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 角力古墳{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}} |所属 = |所在地 = [[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]]牛窓町鹿忍 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 36|緯度秒 = 54.75 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 8|経度秒 = 38.75 |ISO = JP-33 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長84m
高さ7m（後円部・前方部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 鉄製品・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡 = 岡山県指定史跡「鹿歩山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 鹿歩山古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する丘陵（中央右）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。左上の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]、右奥の丘陵に[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]が所在し、中央の丘陵跡に[[波歌山古墳]]が所在した。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''鹿歩山古墳'''（かぶやまこふん/かぶさんこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]]牛窓町鹿忍（かしの）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。岡山県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||'''鹿歩山古墳'''||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓湾の中央西の鹿歩山（標高92.6メートル：築造当時は半島状地形{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}）の山頂部に築造された古墳である。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[牛窓天神山古墳]]・[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られ{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、それらのうちでは最高所に位置する{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。現在では古墳南側に鹿忍神社が鎮座する。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}。 墳形は前方部が大きく発達した前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]（人頭大の礫）・[[埴輪]]片のほか、鉄製品（甲冑片か）が検出されている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされ（牛窓湾周辺の古墳では唯一）、周濠を含めた全長は約100メートルにおよぶ{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は明らかでない（盗掘済か）{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}史跡説明板。[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html 鹿歩山古墳]（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。（または5世紀末{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳と同時期には、長船地域でも周濠を伴う古墳として[[築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|築山古墳]]が築造されており、吉備地方での巨大古墳の消滅や『[[日本書紀]]』の記す[[雄略天皇]]期の吉備地方の動乱との関連性が指摘される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=126-129}}。 古墳域は[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]34年）に岡山県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鹿歩山から牛窓湾を望む}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 古墳総長：約100メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：約84メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約41メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約28メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 長さ：約45メートル ** 幅：約64メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル 墳丘は前方部が発達した形態であり、類似形態の古墳が邑久地域を中心に分布する点が注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。周濠の外側では、前方部側で幅約8メートルの周堤帯が認められるが、後円部側では明瞭さを欠く{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 岡山県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鹿歩山古墳 - 1956年（昭和34年）3月27日指定[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 鹿歩山古墳]（岡山県「おかやまの文化財」）。{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf 指定文化財・登録文化財一覧]}}（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。。 == 鹿忍神社 == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鹿忍神社}}]] 鹿歩山古墳の南側には'''鹿忍神社'''（かしのじんじゃ）が鎮座する。[[祭神]]は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[フツヌシ|布津主命]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]・[[比売神|姫大神]]・山田彦命の5柱。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年（[[1617年]]）の[[棟札]]には[[神護景雲]]年間（[[767年|767]]-[[770年]]）の再建とあるという。当初は稲津明神として祀られていたが、のちに五社大明神に改め、[[明治]]期に鹿忍神社に[[改号]]された。本殿は瀬戸内市指定重要文化財に指定されている。 == 周辺の古墳 == ; 横山1号墳 : 鹿歩山古墳の南約200メートルに所在する[[円墳]]。現状は一部残存。直径6-7メートル、高さ1.5メートル。埋葬施設は無袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、略測で長さ約5メートル、幅1.05メートル、高さ0.40メートルを測る『牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）』 牛窓町、1997年。。2号墳は1号墳の南東約5メートル地点にあったが、[[開墾]]により消滅している{{Cite book |和書 |author= |year=瀬戸内市教育委員会 |title=遺跡一覧表 |page=69 |publisher=瀬戸内市 }}。 ; 京塚古墳 : 鹿歩山古墳の南西に近接する円墳。現状は一部残存。直径22メートル、高さ2メートルとされる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石槨]]と伝わる。 ; 木山様古墳群 : 鹿歩山の南丘陵に所在した円墳群。現在は消滅。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（瀬戸内市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=150-156|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=鹿歩山古墳||url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図]|publisher=牛窓町教育委員会|isbn=|page=86|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|鹿歩山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 鹿歩山古墳] - 岡山県「おかやまの文化財」 * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html 鹿歩山古墳] - 瀬戸内市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:かふやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:岡山県指定史跡]]

高さ7m（後円部・前方部） |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 鉄製品・[[埴輪]]片 |史跡 = 岡山県指定史跡「鹿歩山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Okayama |ラベル = 鹿歩山古墳 |アイコン=前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|古墳の所在する丘陵（中央右）}}{{small|[[牛窓オリーブ園]]から望む。左上の黒島に[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]、右奥の丘陵に[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]が所在し、中央の丘陵跡に[[波歌山古墳]]が所在した。}}]] {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|34|36|30|N|134|9|5|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 300| height =250 | caption = {{center|牛窓湾の前方後円墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|37|0.87|N|134|9|56.77|E}} | label1=牛窓天神山 | label-pos1=bottom | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南南東.png | mark-size1=25 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|35|54.53|N|134|9|35.45|E}} | label2=黒島 | label-pos2=top | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size2=25 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} | label3=鹿歩山 | label-pos3=bottom | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-北東.png | mark-size3=25 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|37|0.0|N|134|9|14.0|E}} | label4=(波歌山) | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size4=25 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|36|7.98|N|134|8|2.17|E}} | label5=二塚山 | label-pos5=right | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後円墳-南東.png | mark-size5=25 }} '''鹿歩山古墳'''（かぶやまこふん/かぶさんこふん）は、[[岡山県]][[瀬戸内市]]牛窓町鹿忍（かしの）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。岡山県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+牛窓湾の前方後円墳5基 !湾内位置!!古墳名!!墳丘長!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |北東||[[牛窓天神山古墳]]||85m||4c中葉-後半||瀬戸内市指定史跡 |- |南||[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]||81m||5c前半-中葉||なし |- |北西||'''鹿歩山古墳'''||84m||5c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |- |北||[[波歌山古墳]]||60m||6c前半||（消滅） |- |南西||[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]||55m||6c後半||岡山県指定史跡 |}
岡山県南東部、牛窓湾の中央西の鹿歩山（標高92.6メートル：築造当時は半島状地形{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}）の山頂部に築造された古墳である。牛窓湾では、本古墳のほか[[二塚山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|二塚山古墳]]・[[波歌山古墳]]（消滅）・[[牛窓天神山古墳]]・[[黒島古墳|黒島1号墳]]の前方後円墳5基が湾を取り囲むように営造されたことが知られ{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}、それらのうちでは最高所に位置する{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}。現在では古墳南側に鹿忍神社が鎮座する。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}。 墳形は前方部が大きく発達した前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]（人頭大の礫）・[[埴輪]]片のほか、鉄製品（甲冑片か）が検出されている{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされ（牛窓湾周辺の古墳では唯一）、周濠を含めた全長は約100メートルにおよぶ{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は明らかでない（盗掘済か）{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}史跡説明板。[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html 鹿歩山古墳]（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。（または5世紀末{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}）頃と推定される。他の前方後円墳4基とともに当時の牛窓湾の港湾としての政治的重要性を示す古墳になる{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。本古墳と同時期には、長船地域でも周濠を伴う古墳として[[築山古墳 (瀬戸内市)|築山古墳]]が築造されており、吉備地方での巨大古墳の消滅や『[[日本書紀]]』の記す[[雄略天皇]]期の吉備地方の動乱との関連性が指摘される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 通史編|2001|pp=126-129}}。 古墳域は[[1956年]]（[[昭和]]34年）に岡山県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鹿歩山から牛窓湾を望む}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 古墳総長：約100メートル - 周濠を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：約84メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：約41メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル * くびれ部 ** 幅：約28メートル ** 高さ：約5メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 長さ：約45メートル ** 幅：約64メートル ** 高さ：約7メートル 墳丘は前方部が発達した形態であり、類似形態の古墳が邑久地域を中心に分布する点が注意される{{Sfn|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997|pp=150-156}}{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。周濠の外側では、前方部側で幅約8メートルの周堤帯が認められるが、後円部側では明瞭さを欠く{{Sfn|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 文化財 == === 岡山県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鹿歩山古墳 - 1956年（昭和34年）3月27日指定[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 鹿歩山古墳]（岡山県「おかやまの文化財」）。{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf 指定文化財・登録文化財一覧]}}（瀬戸内市ホームページ）。。 == 鹿忍神社 == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鹿忍神社}}]] 鹿歩山古墳の南側には'''鹿忍神社'''（かしのじんじゃ）が鎮座する。[[祭神]]は[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[フツヌシ|布津主命]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]・[[比売神|姫大神]]・山田彦命の5柱。[[元和 (日本)|元和]]3年（[[1617年]]）の[[棟札]]には[[神護景雲]]年間（[[767年|767]]-[[770年]]）の再建とあるという。当初は稲津明神として祀られていたが、のちに五社大明神に改め、[[明治]]期に鹿忍神社に[[改号]]された。本殿は瀬戸内市指定重要文化財に指定されている。 == 周辺の古墳 == ; 横山1号墳 : 鹿歩山古墳の南約200メートルに所在する[[円墳]]。現状は一部残存。直径6-7メートル、高さ1.5メートル。埋葬施設は無袖式[[横穴式石室]]で、略測で長さ約5メートル、幅1.05メートル、高さ0.40メートルを測る『牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）』 牛窓町、1997年。。2号墳は1号墳の南東約5メートル地点にあったが、[[開墾]]により消滅している{{Cite book |和書 |author= |year=瀬戸内市教育委員会 |title=遺跡一覧表 |page=69 |publisher=瀬戸内市 }}。 ; 京塚古墳 : 鹿歩山古墳の南西に近接する円墳。現状は一部残存。直径22メートル、高さ2メートルとされる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石槨]]と伝わる。 ; 木山様古墳群 : 鹿歩山の南丘陵に所在した円墳群。現在は消滅。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（瀬戸内市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=牛窓町史 資料編II（考古・古代・中世・近世）|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|pages=150-156|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 資料編II|1997}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=|title=牛窓町史 通史編|publisher=牛窓町|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町史 通史編|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=鹿歩山古墳||url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639 |title=牛窓町古墳図]|publisher=牛窓町教育委員会|isbn=|page=86|ref={{Harvid|牛窓町古墳図|2002}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 34 岡山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490344|ref={{Harvid|鹿歩山古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=草原孝典|year=2002|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鹿歩山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=鹿歩山古墳|title=岡山県史 一八 考古資料|publisher=岡山県|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 鹿歩山古墳] - 岡山県「おかやまの文化財」 * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html 鹿歩山古墳] - 瀬戸内市ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:かふやまこふん}} [[Category:岡山県の古墳]] [[Category:瀬戸内市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:岡山県指定史跡]]

Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=draft}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|12=draft}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Kabuyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{citation needed|Kabuyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}

The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|ja|瀬戸内市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setouchi, Okayama|qq=}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Himegami|ja|比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kabuyama Kofun]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height.
