Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.615208|N|134.144097|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228158]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location=[[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]] Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments |built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, [[:en:Himegami|Himegami]], and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}

Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments |built=Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Setouchi, Okayama|Setouchi, Okayama]], Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, [[:en:Himegami|Himegami]], and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.615208|N|134.144097|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228158]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.615208|N|134.144097|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.615208|N|134.144097|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Kabuyama Kofun |image=[[File:Kabuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (front section on the right, rear circular section in the back left) |alternate_name=Sumo Kofun{{Sfn|Ushimado Town History Materials II|1997|pp=150-156}} |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}} Ushimado Town, Kashino |coordinates={{Coord|34|36|54.75|N|134|8|38.75|E}} |type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |map_size=Length: 84m
Height: 7m (both rear and front sections) |Burial Facility=Unknown |burial goods=Iron products, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} fragments |built=Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1956 |image=[[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Ushimado Olive Garden-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hill where the kofun is located (center right)}}{{small|Seen from Ushimado Olive Garden. Top left: {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}; back right: {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; center hill: former site of {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}.}}]] }} '''Kabuyama Kofun''' (鹿歩山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Setouchi, Okayama|en|Setouchi, Okayama|WD=Q691691|lt=Setouchi, Okayama}}, Ushimado Town, Kashino, Okayama Prefecture. It is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ Keyhole-shaped tumuli in Ushimado Bay !location within bay!!name!!Length!!built!!Historic Site Designation |- |Northeast||{{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}||85m||Mid to late 4th century||Setouchi City Historic Site |- |South||{{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}} (No.1)||81m||Early to mid-5th century||None |- |Northwest||'''Kabuyama Kofun'''||84m||Late 5th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |- |North||{{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}}||60m||Early 6th century||Demolished |- |Southwest||{{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||55m||Late 6th century||Okayama Prefecture Historic Site |}
Kabuyama Kofun is located at the summit of Kabuyama Hill (92.6 meters above sea level), which during its construction resembled a peninsula. The tumulus is one of five keyhole-shaped tumuli encircling Ushimado Bay, including {{ill|Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi City)|lt=Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|二塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsukayama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namikayama Kofun|ja|波歌山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namikayama Kofun|qq=}} (now destroyed), {{ill|Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|牛窓天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushimado Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kuroshima Kofun|ja|黒島古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroshima Kofun|qq=}}. Kabuyama Kofun is situated at the highest elevation among these and currently neighbors Kashino Shrine to the south. No archaeological excavations have been conducted to date. The kofun has a distinct keyhole shape with a prominently developed front section facing northeast. The mound is constructed in three tiers. Surface findings include paving stones (large cobbles), fragments of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, and iron artifacts, possibly parts of armor. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped moat, the only one of its kind among the Ushimado tumuli. Including the moat, the total length of the kofun reaches approximately 100 meters. The burial facility remains unidentified and may have been looted. The kofun dates to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, specifically the late 5th century or possibly the end of that century. Alongside other tumuli, it highlights the political importance of Ushimado Bay as a port during this era. The contemporaneous {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|ja|築山古墳_(瀬戸内市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Setouchi city)|qq=}} in the Osafune region also features a surrounding moat, linking its construction to the broader historical context of conflicts in the Kibi region, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} during the reign of Emperor Yūryaku. The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/kurashi/kankorekishi/rekishi/jyuyobunkazai/shisekikoukou/kenjyuyo/1418023554566.html Kabuyama Kofun] - Setouchi City official site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鹿歩山古墳|version=95137441|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿歩山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17228158}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Setouchi, Okayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Okayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

The kofun was designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1956. == Mound Details == [[File:Ushimado-bay viewed from Kabuyama.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|View of Ushimado Bay from Kabuyama}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: approximately 100 meters (including moat) * Mound length: approximately 84 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: approximately 41 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters * Neck section: ** Width: approximately 28 meters ** Height: approximately 5 meters * Front rectangular section: ** Length: approximately 45 meters ** Width: approximately 64 meters ** Height: approximately 7 meters The mound’s distinctive development of the front section is notable, with similar shapes observed in tumuli around the Oku area. Outside the moat, the front section features a moat bank approximately 8 meters wide, though it is less defined on the rear circular section side. == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabuyama Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1956[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.setouchi.lg.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/91/bunkazai_itiran(20161001).pdf List of Designated Cultural Properties]}} (Setouchi City official site).. == Kashino Shrine == [[File:Kashino-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Kashino Shrine}}]] South of Kabuyama Kofun lies Kashino Shrine (鹿忍神社). The shrine venerates five deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|布津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Himegami|en|Himegami|WD=Q22070227|lt=Himegami|1=Himegami}}, and Yamadahiko no Mikoto. A ridgepole inscription from 1617 notes a rebuilding during the Jingo Keiun era (767–770). Originally called Inazu Myōjin, it was later renamed Gosha Daimyōjin and then Kashino Shrine during the Meiji period. Its main shrine is designated as a Setouchi City Important Cultural Property. == Surrounding Tumuli == * '''Yokoyama No. 1 Kofun''': Located about 200 meters south of Kabuyama Kofun, this {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} measures 6–7 meters in diameter and 1.5 meters in height. Its burial facility is a single-sided horizontal stone chamber measuring approximately 5 meters in length, 1.05 meters in width, and 0.40 meters in height. The No. 2 Kofun, located 5 meters southeast of No. 1, has been destroyed. * '''Kyōzuka Kofun''': Located southwest of Kabuyama Kofun, this round tumulus has a diameter of 22 meters and a height of 2 meters. The burial facility is said to be a pit-style stone chamber. * '''Kiyama-sama Kofun Cluster''': A cluster of round tumuli located on the southern slopes of Kabuyama, now destroyed. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for the article)}} * Explanatory panel at the site (Setouchi City Board of Education) * Publications by local authorities ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1997|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Ushimado Town History Materials II: Archaeology, Ancient, Medieval, and Early Modern|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=|pages=150-156}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2001|title=Ushimado Town History|publisher=Ushimado Town|isbn=}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/12639|title=Ushimado Town Ancient Kofuns Chart|publisher=Ushimado Town Board of Education|page=86}} * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia, Volume 34: Okayama Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490344}} ''' {{Cite book|editor=|author=Takanori Kusahara|year=2002|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Continued Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|isbn=4490105991}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=Kabuyama Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture History 18: Archaeological Materials|publisher=Okayama Prefecture|isbn=}} == External Links == * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=361 Kabuyama Kofun] -
